meaning: v.摇晃,蹒跚,交错,摇摇摆摆adj.交错的
examples: There were so many runners that they had to stagger the start.
I managed to stagger the last few steps.
We seem to stagger from one crisis to the next.
Especially when we realise that gasp-inducing Italian super-deli Valvona and Crolla (valvonacrolla.co.uk) is only a stagger away.
He burns with hope, which is why grown men stagger blinking from his optimism.
